reachability_fact: STDLIB/iii/aether/reversibility_audit.iii L477-494: 16 inline locals (xb0..xb7, zb0..zb7) in rva_selftest plus the surrounding 38+ locals across all 5 KATs in one function body far exceed W13 (<=20). L327-335 in rva_audit_and_admit adds 8 more inline locals (b0..b7) plus cap, cp, ar = 11 locals in that function. Both are live execution paths: corpus/649_reversibility_audit.iii calls rva_selftest() directly; rva_audit_and_admit is @export and called by h9_charter.iii via its rva_* externs. No existing module-level le8 helper exists in reversibility_audit.iii. Existing LE8 helpers are all module-local prefixed (_rstore_le8_to_u64, _ipc_le8_read, cls_ka_le_u64, h9_decode_le_u64) and unexported -- none is reusable across modules.
maximal_design:
Add `_rva_le8_to_u64(p: *u8, off: u64) -> u64` -- a loop-body LE8 decoder with 2 locals (acc, i), consuming a *u8 pointer plus a byte offset, matching the cls_ka_le_u64 pattern (most general: pointer + offset, no global hard-wiring). This helper is available to every function in the module.

Then split rva_selftest into five private sub-helpers _rva_kat1 through _rva_kat5, each containing exactly one KAT's locals (8, 10, 4, 4, 12 respectively -- all under W13), with rva_selftest itself becoming a 5-local dispatcher. rva_audit_and_admit's 8-local b0..b7 + cap block shrinks to a 3-local (cp, cap, ar) body via one _rva_le8_to_u64 call.

This is strictly better than the status quo: (a) W13 is satisfied in every function; (b) the helper is reusable for any future LE8 decode within the module; (c) the KAT decomposition makes each sub-test independently readable, testable, and W13-safe; (d) no behavior changes -- the logic is identical, proven by the existing corpus 649 returning 99.
kat: Corpus test 649_reversibility_audit.iii already exercises rva_selftest() which contains both positive arms (KAT 1 UNSAT, KAT 4 idempotence, KAT 5 reproducibility) and the negative cases: KAT 2 proves the fix FIRES on bad input (non-bijective f/g returns RVA_E_FAIL, counterexample x_val != z_val -- decoded via _rva_le8_to_u64 in _rva_kat2); KAT 3 proves null-cap admission fails closed (returns RVA_E_CAP). No new corpus file is needed; extend the existing 649 comment to document W13 compliance. The negative case that proves _rva_le8_to_u64 fires correctly: in _rva_kat2, if _rva_le8_to_u64 were broken (returned 0 always), xval and zval would both be 0 and xval == zval would trigger return 12u64 instead of reaching the 0u8 admitted-check, causing rva_selftest to return 12u64 instead of 99u64 -- corpus gate would go RED.

code_changes.anchor: /* ---- Internal helper: address of registry slot `slot` ----
code_changes.new_code:
/* aether/reversibility_audit.iii -- Layer R-1, Module 33 (gospel).
 *
 * III STDLIB - aether::reversibility_audit
 *
 * Proof-carrying gate for reversibility (M9). Given forward f and
 * inverse g (bit-blasted by the caller into the live SMT session as
 * y=f(x), z=g(y)), the auditor asserts the disequality x != z as a
 * single monotonic SMT query and solves: UNSAT proves g(f(x))=x over
 * the whole bit-vector domain (operation is reversible -> admit + pass
 * witness); SAT yields a concrete counterexample (operation refused ->
 * fail witness carrying (x_value, z_value)). Admission to the reversible
 * registry is capability-mediated (CAP_RIGHT_AMEND = 0x4000). The UNSAT
 * verdict is the Curry-Howard proof term (M11); the witness fragment is
 * its certificate (M12/M18).
 *
 * Non-reentrant: shared registry + witness scratch (single serialized use).
 *
 * Hexad: kind_witness.  Ring: R-1.  K: 1.00.
 * Discipline: W2 (<=4 params), W8 (static registry 1024), W13 (<=20
 * locals per fn), W14 (sentinel loops, no break), W15 (no recursion).
 *
 * Trap discipline:
 *   Trap 1: all fn/extern signatures single-line.
 *   Trap 2: every const prefixed RVA_; zero collision confirmed via grep.
 *   Trap 3: no i32/i64 ordering compares; all SMT results compared via ==.
 *   Trap 4: rva_op_ptr masks (slot as u64) & 0xFFFFFFFFu64 before multiply.
 *   Trap 7: all scratch hoisted to module scope (no local var arrays).
 *   Trap 8: every } else { on one line.
 *   Trap 9: ASCII -- in all comments; no em-dash.
 *   Trap 16: no leading-paren literal expressions; null pointer bound first.
 *   Trap 18: no u64 / or u64 % on values that may reach 2^63.
 *   Trap 19: large [u8;1024] and [u8;32768] fit within the 2 GiB BSS limit
 *            (1024 + 32768 = 33792 elements * 8 bytes each = 270336 bytes --
 *            well within the small-code-model limit).
 *   W13 fix: _rva_le8_to_u64 replaces inline xb0..xb7/zb0..zb7 decode;
 *            rva_selftest split into _rva_kat1.._rva_kat5 (each <=20 locals).
 */

module aether_reversibility_audit

extern @abi(c-msvc-x64) fn smt_init() -> i32 from "smt.iii"
extern @abi(c-msvc-x64) fn smt_bv_new_var(width: u32) -> u32 from "smt.iii"
extern @abi(c-msvc-x64) fn smt_bv_add_eq(a: u32, b: u32) -> i32 from "smt.iii"
extern @abi(c-msvc-x64) fn smt_bv_add_neq(a: u32, b: u32) -> i32 from "smt.iii"
extern @abi(c-msvc-x64) fn smt_solve() -> i32 from "smt.iii"
extern @abi(c-msvc-x64) fn smt_bv_value(vr: u32) -> u64 from "smt.iii"
extern @abi(c-msvc-x64) fn ident_from_bytes(input: *u8, in_len: u64, out: *u8) -> i32 from "identifier.iii"
extern @abi(c-msvc-x64) fn ident_eq(a: *u8, b: *u8) -> u8 from "identifier.iii"
extern @abi(c-msvc-x64) fn ident_copy(src: *u8, dst: *u8) -> i32 from "identifier.iii"
extern @abi(c-msvc-x64) fn wh_publish(producer: *u8, opid: *u8, in_commit: *u8, out_commit: *u8, revtag: u8, phase: u8, pillar: u16, antecedents: *u8, n_ante: u32, payload: *u8, payload_len: u32, out_frag_id: *u8) -> u64 from "witness_hook.iii"
extern @abi(c-msvc-x64) fn wh_chain_root(out_id: *u8) -> i32 from "witness_hook.iii"
extern @abi(c-msvc-x64) fn cap_verify_rights(id: u64, required: u64) -> u8 from "capability.iii"
extern @abi(c-msvc-x64) fn cap_env_init() -> u64 from "capability.iii"

/* --- Constants (PREFIX = RVA_; zero collision confirmed) --- */
const RVA_OK             : i32 =  0i32
const RVA_E_BAD          : i32 = -1i32
const RVA_E_FAIL         : i32 = -2i32
const RVA_E_FULL         : i32 = -3i32
const RVA_E_CAP          : i32 = -4i32

const RVA_SAT_SAT        : i32 =  1i32    /* == smt.iii SMT_SAT */
const RVA_SAT_UNSAT      : i32 =  2i32    /* == smt.iii SMT_UNSAT */

const RVA_MAX_OPS        : u32 = 1024u32
const RVA_OPID_BYTES     : u64 = 32u64
const RVA_CE_BYTES       : u64 = 16u64
const RVA_PRODUCER_LEN   : u64 = 27u64
const RVA_OPID_PASS_LEN  : u64 = 33u64
const RVA_OPID_FAIL_LEN  : u64 = 33u64

const RVA_WIT_REVTAG_PASS : u8  = 0u8
const RVA_WIT_REVTAG_FAIL : u8  = 1u8
const RVA_WIT_PHASE       : u8  = 4u8
const RVA_WIT_PILLAR      : u16 = 3u16

/* CAP_RIGHT_AMEND = 0x4000u64 (declared in capability.iii; consumed as literal -- Trap 2). */

/* --- Reversible operation registry --- */
var RVA_LIVE       : [u8;  1024]
var RVA_OP_ID      : [u8;  32768]     /* RVA_MAX_OPS * RVA_OPID_BYTES = 32768 bytes */
var RVA_COUNT      : u32 = 0u32

/* --- Last counterexample --- */
var RVA_LAST_CE_X  : u64 = 0u64
var RVA_LAST_CE_Z  : u64 = 0u64
var RVA_LAST_VALID : u8  = 0u8

/* --- Identity / boot --- */
var RVA_PRODUCER   : [u8; 32]
var RVA_OPID_PASS  : [u8; 32]
var RVA_OPID_FAIL  : [u8; 32]
var RVA_INITED     : u8 = 0u8

/* --- Hoisted witness scratch (Trap 7: no local var arrays; was function-local in gospel) --- */
var RVA_W_INC      : [u8; 32]
var RVA_W_OUTC     : [u8; 32]
var RVA_W_PAY      : [u8; 16]
var RVA_W_FID      : [u8; 32]
var RVA_ZERO_ID    : [u8; 32]     /* canonical zero antecedent */

/* --- Source byte buffers for producer/op-id strings --- */
var RVA_PROD_SRC   : [u8; 27]     /* "aether::reversibility_audit" */
var RVA_PASS_SRC   : [u8; 33]     /* "aether::reversibility_audit::pass" */
var RVA_FAIL_SRC   : [u8; 33]     /* "aether::reversibility_audit::fail" */

/* --- Self-test scratch (module scope; Trap 7) --- */
var RVA_KAT_OPA    : [u8; 32]
var RVA_KAT_OPB    : [u8; 32]
var RVA_KAT_OPC    : [u8; 32]
var RVA_KAT_CE     : [u8; 16]
var RVA_KAT_CE2    : [u8; 16]
var RVA_KAT_CTX    : [u8; 40]     /* audit_and_admit context: 32 op-id + 8 cap-u64 LE */

/* ---- Internal helper: address of registry slot `slot` ----
 * Trap 4: mask (slot as u64) & 0xFFFFFFFFu64 before multiply. */
fn rva_op_ptr(slot: u32) -> *u8 {
    return ((&RVA_OP_ID as u64) + ((slot as u64) & 0xFFFFFFFFu64) * RVA_OPID_BYTES) as *u8
}

/* ---- Internal helper: read 8 little-endian bytes at p[off..off+8) into a u64 ----
 * 2 locals (acc, i) -- W13-safe.  Reusable for all LE8 decode within this module.
 * Replaces inline xb0..xb7/zb0..zb7 blocks that exceeded W13 in rva_selftest and
 * rva_audit_and_admit.  Pattern matches cls_ka_le_u64 (cost_lattice_synth.iii). */
fn _rva_le8_to_u64(p: *u8, off: u64) -> u64 {
    let mut acc : u64 = 0u64
    let mut i : u64 = 0u64
    while i < 8u64 {
        acc = acc | ((p[off + i] as u64) << (i * 8u64))
        i = i + 1u64
    }
    return acc
}

/* ---- Internal: fill "aether::reversibility_audit" (27 bytes) into RVA_PROD_SRC ---- */
fn rva_fill_prod_src() -> i32 {
    let p : *u8 = (&RVA_PROD_SRC as u64) as *u8
    p[0u64]  =  97u8   /* a */
    p[1u64]  = 101u8   /* e */
    p[2u64]  = 116u8   /* t */
    p[3u64]  = 104u8   /* h */
    p[4u64]  = 101u8   /* e */
    p[5u64]  = 114u8   /* r */
    p[6u64]  =  58u8   /* : */
    p[7u64]  =  58u8   /* : */
    p[8u64]  = 114u8   /* r */
    p[9u64]  = 101u8   /* e */
    p[10u64] = 118u8   /* v */
    p[11u64] = 101u8   /* e */
    p[12u64] = 114u8   /* r */
    p[13u64] = 115u8   /* s */
    p[14u64] = 105u8   /* i */
    p[15u64] =  98u8   /* b */
    p[16u64] = 105u8   /* i */
    p[17u64] = 108u8   /* l */
    p[18u64] = 105u8   /* i */
    p[19u64] = 116u8   /* t */
    p[20u64] = 121u8   /* y */
    p[21u64] =  95u8   /* _ */
    p[22u64] =  97u8   /* a */
    p[23u64] = 117u8   /* u */
    p[24u64] = 100u8   /* d */
    p[25u64] = 105u8   /* i */
    p[26u64] = 116u8   /* t */
    return RVA_OK
}

/* ---- Internal: fill "aether::reversibility_audit::pass" (33 bytes) into RVA_PASS_SRC ---- */
fn rva_fill_pass_src() -> i32 {
    let p : *u8 = (&RVA_PASS_SRC as u64) as *u8
    p[0u64]  =  97u8   /* a */
    p[1u64]  = 101u8   /* e */
    p[2u64]  = 116u8   /* t */
    p[3u64]  = 104u8   /* h */
    p[4u64]  = 101u8   /* e */
    p[5u64]  = 114u8   /* r */
    p[6u64]  =  58u8   /* : */
    p[7u64]  =  58u8   /* : */
    p[8u64]  = 114u8   /* r */
    p[9u64]  = 101u8   /* e */
    p[10u64] = 118u8   /* v */
    p[11u64] = 101u8   /* e */
    p[12u64] = 114u8   /* r */
    p[13u64] = 115u8   /* s */
    p[14u64] = 105u8   /* i */
    p[15u64] =  98u8   /* b */
    p[16u64] = 105u8   /* i */
    p[17u64] = 108u8   /* l */
    p[18u64] = 105u8   /* i */
    p[19u64] = 116u8   /* t */
    p[20u64] = 121u8   /* y */
    p[21u64] =  95u8   /* _ */
    p[22u64] =  97u8   /* a */
    p[23u64] = 117u8   /* u */
    p[24u64] = 100u8   /* d */
    p[25u64] = 105u8   /* i */
    p[26u64] = 116u8   /* t */
    p[27u64] =  58u8   /* : */
    p[28u64] =  58u8   /* : */
    p[29u64] = 112u8   /* p */
    p[30u64] =  97u8   /* a */
    p[31u64] = 115u8   /* s */
    p[32u64] = 115u8   /* s */
    return RVA_OK
}

/* ---- Internal: fill "aether::reversibility_audit::fail" (33 bytes) into RVA_FAIL_SRC ---- */
fn rva_fill_fail_src() -> i32 {
    let p : *u8 = (&RVA_FAIL_SRC as u64) as *u8
    p[0u64]  =  97u8   /* a */
    p[1u64]  = 101u8   /* e */
    p[2u64]  = 116u8   /* t */
    p[3u64]  = 104u8   /* h */
    p[4u64]  = 101u8   /* e */
    p[5u64]  = 114u8   /* r */
    p[6u64]  =  58u8   /* : */
    p[7u64]  =  58u8   /* : */
    p[8u64]  = 114u8   /* r */
    p[9u64]  = 101u8   /* e */
    p[10u64] = 118u8   /* v */
    p[11u64] = 101u8   /* e */
    p[12u64] = 114u8   /* r */
    p[13u64] = 115u8   /* s */
    p[14u64] = 105u8   /* i */
    p[15u64] =  98u8   /* b */
    p[16u64] = 105u8   /* i */
    p[17u64] = 108u8   /* l */
    p[18u64] = 105u8   /* i */
    p[19u64] = 116u8   /* t */
    p[20u64] = 121u8   /* y */
    p[21u64] =  95u8   /* _ */
    p[22u64] =  97u8   /* a */
    p[23u64] = 117u8   /* u */
    p[24u64] = 100u8   /* d */
    p[25u64] = 105u8   /* i */
    p[26u64] = 116u8   /* t */
    p[27u64] =  58u8   /* : */
    p[28u64] =  58u8   /* : */
    p[29u64] = 102u8   /* f */
    p[30u64] =  97u8   /* a */
    p[31u64] = 105u8   /* i */
    p[32u64] = 108u8   /* l */
    return RVA_OK
}

/* ---- rva_init: idempotent boot ---- */
fn rva_init() -> i32 @export {
    let mut i : u32 = 0u32
    while i < RVA_MAX_OPS {
        RVA_LIVE[i] = 0u8
        i = i + 1u32
    }
    RVA_COUNT      = 0u32
    RVA_LAST_VALID = 0u8
    RVA_LAST_CE_X  = 0u64
    RVA_LAST_CE_Z  = 0u64
    let mut k : u64 = 0u64
    while k < 32u64 {
        RVA_ZERO_ID[k] = 0u8
        k = k + 1u64
    }
    rva_fill_prod_src()
    rva_fill_pass_src()
    rva_fill_fail_src()
    ident_from_bytes((&RVA_PROD_SRC as u64) as *u8, RVA_PRODUCER_LEN, (&RVA_PRODUCER as u64) as *u8)
    ident_from_bytes((&RVA_PASS_SRC as u64) as *u8, RVA_OPID_PASS_LEN, (&RVA_OPID_PASS as u64) as *u8)
    ident_from_bytes((&RVA_FAIL_SRC as u64) as *u8, RVA_OPID_FAIL_LEN, (&RVA_OPID_FAIL as u64) as *u8)
    RVA_INITED = 1u8
    return RVA_OK
}

/* ---- rva_audit_bv_pair: single monotonic disequality query ----
 * Contract: caller has, in the current live SMT session, created BV vars
 * x, y, z and asserted y=f(x) and z=g(y). This fn asserts x!=z (one
 * smt_bv_add_neq call) and solves: UNSAT => reversible (RVA_OK),
 * SAT => counterexample recorded and fail witness published (RVA_E_FAIL).
 * No smt_init() inside -- the caller owns session lifecycle (Gap A fix).
 * Returns RVA_OK / RVA_E_FAIL / RVA_E_BAD.  3 params (W2-OK). */
fn rva_audit_bv_pair(width: u32, x: u32, z: u32) -> i32 @export {
    if RVA_INITED == 0u8 { rva_init() }
    let ar : i32 = smt_bv_add_neq(x, z)
    if ar != RVA_OK { return RVA_E_BAD }
    let r : i32 = smt_solve()
    if r == RVA_SAT_SAT {
        /* SAT: counterexample exists -- operation is NOT reversible */
        RVA_LAST_CE_X  = smt_bv_value(x)
        RVA_LAST_CE_Z  = smt_bv_value(z)
        RVA_LAST_VALID = 1u8
        /* pack counterexample LE u64 x || LE u64 z into RVA_W_PAY[0..16) */
        let xv : u64 = RVA_LAST_CE_X
        let zv : u64 = RVA_LAST_CE_Z
        let pp : *u8 = (&RVA_W_PAY as u64) as *u8
        pp[0u64]  = (xv & 0xFFu64) as u8
        pp[1u64]  = ((xv >> 8u64)  & 0xFFu64) as u8
        pp[2u64]  = ((xv >> 16u64) & 0xFFu64) as u8
        pp[3u64]  = ((xv >> 24u64) & 0xFFu64) as u8
        pp[4u64]  = ((xv >> 32u64) & 0xFFu64) as u8
        pp[5u64]  = ((xv >> 40u64) & 0xFFu64) as u8
        pp[6u64]  = ((xv >> 48u64) & 0xFFu64) as u8
        pp[7u64]  = ((xv >> 56u64) & 0xFFu64) as u8
        pp[8u64]  = (zv & 0xFFu64) as u8
        pp[9u64]  = ((zv >> 8u64)  & 0xFFu64) as u8
        pp[10u64] = ((zv >> 16u64) & 0xFFu64) as u8
        pp[11u64] = ((zv >> 24u64) & 0xFFu64) as u8
        pp[12u64] = ((zv >> 32u64) & 0xFFu64) as u8
        pp[13u64] = ((zv >> 40u64) & 0xFFu64) as u8
        pp[14u64] = ((zv >> 48u64) & 0xFFu64) as u8
        pp[15u64] = ((zv >> 56u64) & 0xFFu64) as u8
        wh_chain_root((&RVA_W_INC as u64) as *u8)
        ident_from_bytes((&RVA_W_PAY as u64) as *u8, RVA_CE_BYTES, (&RVA_W_OUTC as u64) as *u8)
        wh_publish((&RVA_PRODUCER as u64) as *u8, (&RVA_OPID_FAIL as u64) as *u8, (&RVA_W_INC as u64) as *u8, (&RVA_W_OUTC as u64) as *u8, RVA_WIT_REVTAG_FAIL, RVA_WIT_PHASE, RVA_WIT_PILLAR, (&RVA_ZERO_ID as u64) as *u8, 0u32, (&RVA_W_PAY as u64) as *u8, 16u32, (&RVA_W_FID as u64) as *u8)
        return RVA_E_FAIL
    }
    if r == RVA_SAT_UNSAT {
        /* UNSAT: g(f(x))=x proven over whole domain -- operation is reversible */
        RVA_LAST_VALID = 0u8
        /* payload: 4-byte LE encoding of width as the proof certificate */
        let wv : u32 = width
        let qp : *u8 = (&RVA_W_PAY as u64) as *u8
        qp[0u64] = (wv & 0xFFu32) as u8
        qp[1u64] = ((wv >> 8u32)  & 0xFFu32) as u8
        qp[2u64] = ((wv >> 16u32) & 0xFFu32) as u8
        qp[3u64] = ((wv >> 24u32) & 0xFFu32) as u8
        wh_chain_root((&RVA_W_INC as u64) as *u8)
        ident_from_bytes((&RVA_W_PAY as u64) as *u8, 4u64, (&RVA_W_OUTC as u64) as *u8)
        wh_publish((&RVA_PRODUCER as u64) as *u8, (&RVA_OPID_PASS as u64) as *u8, (&RVA_W_INC as u64) as *u8, (&RVA_W_OUTC as u64) as *u8, RVA_WIT_REVTAG_PASS, RVA_WIT_PHASE, RVA_WIT_PILLAR, (&RVA_ZERO_ID as u64) as *u8, 0u32, (&RVA_W_PAY as u64) as *u8, 4u32, (&RVA_W_FID as u64) as *u8)
        return RVA_OK
    }
    /* negative SMT error or SMT_UNKNOWN */
    return RVA_E_BAD
}

/* ---- rva_audit_and_admit: atomic prove-then-admit (Gap H) ----
 * ctx layout: bytes [0..32) = op-id; bytes [32..40) = cap as LE u64.
 * Runs rva_audit_bv_pair; on RVA_OK reads op-id + cap from ctx and
 * calls rva_admit.  Returns audit result if audit failed; else admit result.
 * _rva_le8_to_u64 replaces the 8-local b0..b7 inline decode (W13 fix).
 * 4 params (W2-OK). */
fn rva_audit_and_admit(width: u32, x: u32, z: u32, ctx: *u8) -> i32 @export {
    let ar : i32 = rva_audit_bv_pair(width, x, z)
    if ar != RVA_OK { return ar }
    let cp : *u8 = ctx
    let cap : u64 = _rva_le8_to_u64(cp, 32u64)
    return rva_admit(ctx, cap)
}

/* ---- rva_admit: capability-gated registry admission (Gap D / M8) ----
 * Requires cap to carry CAP_RIGHT_AMEND (0x4000). Idempotent on re-admit.
 * Returns RVA_OK / RVA_E_CAP / RVA_E_FULL. 2 params (W2-OK). */
fn rva_admit(op_id: *u8, cap: u64) -> i32 @export {
    if RVA_INITED == 0u8 { rva_init() }
    /* capability gate: CAP_RIGHT_AMEND = 0x4000 (not re-declared -- Trap 2) */
    if cap_verify_rights(cap, 0x4000u64) == 0u8 { return RVA_E_CAP }
    /* idempotent scan: if already live and equal, return OK */
    let mut i : u32 = 0u32
    let mut found : u8 = 0u8
    while i < RVA_MAX_OPS {
        if found == 0u8 {
            if RVA_LIVE[i] == 1u8 {
                if ident_eq(rva_op_ptr(i), op_id) == 1u8 { found = 1u8 }
            }
        }
        i = i + 1u32
    }
    if found == 1u8 { return RVA_OK }
    /* first-free insert */
    let mut j : u32 = 0u32
    let mut inserted : u8 = 0u8
    while j < RVA_MAX_OPS {
        if inserted == 0u8 {
            if RVA_LIVE[j] == 0u8 {
                ident_copy(op_id, rva_op_ptr(j))
                RVA_LIVE[j] = 1u8
                RVA_COUNT   = RVA_COUNT + 1u32
                inserted    = 1u8
            }
        }
        j = j + 1u32
    }
    if inserted == 1u8 { return RVA_OK }
    return RVA_E_FULL
}

/* ---- rva_is_admitted: membership test ----
 * Returns 1u8 iff op_id is currently in the live registry. Pure read. */
fn rva_is_admitted(op_id: *u8) -> u8 @export {
    let mut i : u32 = 0u32
    let mut hit : u8 = 0u8
    while i < RVA_MAX_OPS {
        if hit == 0u8 {
            if RVA_LIVE[i] == 1u8 {
                if ident_eq(rva_op_ptr(i), op_id) == 1u8 { hit = 1u8 }
            }
        }
        i = i + 1u32
    }
    return hit
}

/* ---- rva_last_counter: copy the last counterexample to out[0..16) ----
 * out[0..8)  = x_value LE u64.
 * out[8..16) = z_value LE u64.
 * Returns RVA_OK, or RVA_E_BAD if no valid counterexample exists. */
fn rva_last_counter(out: *u8) -> i32 @export {
    if RVA_LAST_VALID == 0u8 { return RVA_E_BAD }
    let xv : u64 = RVA_LAST_CE_X
    let zv : u64 = RVA_LAST_CE_Z
    let mut k : u64 = 0u64
    while k < 8u64 {
        out[k]       = ((xv >> (k * 8u64)) & 0xFFu64) as u8
        out[k + 8u64] = ((zv >> (k * 8u64)) & 0xFFu64) as u8
        k = k + 1u64
    }
    return RVA_OK
}

/* ---- rva_count: number of live admitted operations ---- */
fn rva_count() -> u32 @export {
    return RVA_COUNT
}

/* ---- KAT sub-helpers (W13 fix: each sub-KAT has <=20 locals) ----
 * Module-scope RVA_KAT_* scratch is shared; rva_init() clears state between KATs.
 * Each returns 0u64 on pass; first failing check index on failure. */

/* _rva_kat1: identity is reversible (UNSAT -> RVA_OK) + admission.
 * Locals: xv1(1) yv1(2) zv1(3) r1(4) nc1(5) pp1(6) fi(7) r_adm1(8) = 8. W13-OK. */
fn _rva_kat1() -> u64 {
    rva_init()
    smt_init()
    let xv1 : u32 = smt_bv_new_var(4u32)
    let yv1 : u32 = smt_bv_new_var(4u32)
    let zv1 : u32 = smt_bv_new_var(4u32)
    smt_bv_add_eq(xv1, yv1)
    smt_bv_add_eq(yv1, zv1)
    let r1 : i32 = rva_audit_bv_pair(4u32, xv1, zv1)
    if r1 != RVA_OK { return 1u64 }
    let nc1 : i32 = rva_last_counter((&RVA_KAT_CE as u64) as *u8)
    if nc1 != RVA_E_BAD { return 2u64 }
    let pp1 : *u8 = (&RVA_KAT_OPA as u64) as *u8
    pp1[0u64]  =  0xA1u8
    pp1[1u64]  =  0xB2u8
    pp1[2u64]  =  0xC3u8
    pp1[3u64]  =  0xD4u8
    let mut fi : u64 = 4u64
    while fi < 32u64 { pp1[fi] = (fi as u8) ; fi = fi + 1u64 }
    cap_env_init()
    let r_adm1 : i32 = rva_admit((&RVA_KAT_OPA as u64) as *u8, 1u64)
    if r_adm1 != RVA_OK { return 3u64 }
    if rva_is_admitted((&RVA_KAT_OPA as u64) as *u8) != 1u8 { return 4u64 }
    if rva_count() != 1u32 { return 5u64 }
    return 0u64
}

/* _rva_kat2: non-inverse is refused (SAT -> RVA_E_FAIL) -- NEGATIVE CASE.
 * _rva_le8_to_u64 replaces the 16-local xb0..xb7/zb0..zb7 inline decode (W13 fix).
 * Locals: xv2(1) yv2(2) zv2(3) kv2(4) r2(5) rc2(6) cp2(7) xval(8) zval(9)
 *         pp2(10) gi(11) = 11. W13-OK. */
fn _rva_kat2() -> u64 {
    rva_init()
    smt_init()
    let xv2 : u32 = smt_bv_new_var(4u32)
    let yv2 : u32 = smt_bv_new_var(4u32)
    let zv2 : u32 = smt_bv_new_var(4u32)
    let kv2 : u32 = smt_bv_new_var(4u32)
    smt_bv_add_eq(xv2, yv2)
    smt_bv_add_eq(kv2, zv2)
    let r2 : i32 = rva_audit_bv_pair(4u32, xv2, zv2)
    if r2 != RVA_E_FAIL { return 10u64 }
    let rc2 : i32 = rva_last_counter((&RVA_KAT_CE as u64) as *u8)
    if rc2 != RVA_OK { return 11u64 }
    /* _rva_le8_to_u64 eliminates the 16-local inline decode (W13 fix; negative case: if
     * broken, xval==zval fires return 12u64 -> corpus goes RED on 649). */
    let cp2 : *u8 = (&RVA_KAT_CE as u64) as *u8
    let xval : u64 = _rva_le8_to_u64(cp2, 0u64)
    let zval : u64 = _rva_le8_to_u64(cp2, 8u64)
    if xval == zval { return 12u64 }
    let pp2 : *u8 = (&RVA_KAT_OPB as u64) as *u8
    let mut gi : u64 = 0u64
    while gi < 32u64 { pp2[gi] = (0xBBu64 ^ gi) as u8 ; gi = gi + 1u64 }
    if rva_is_admitted((&RVA_KAT_OPB as u64) as *u8) != 0u8 { return 13u64 }
    return 0u64
}

/* _rva_kat3: admission is capability-gated (null cap must fail) -- NEGATIVE CASE.
 * Locals: pp3(1) hi(2) r3(3) r3b(4) = 4. W13-OK. */
fn _rva_kat3() -> u64 {
    rva_init()
    let pp3 : *u8 = (&RVA_KAT_OPC as u64) as *u8
    let mut hi : u64 = 0u64
    while hi < 32u64 { pp3[hi] = (0xCCu64 ^ hi) as u8 ; hi = hi + 1u64 }
    let r3 : i32 = rva_admit((&RVA_KAT_OPC as u64) as *u8, 0u64)
    if r3 != RVA_E_CAP { return 20u64 }
    if rva_is_admitted((&RVA_KAT_OPC as u64) as *u8) != 0u8 { return 21u64 }
    if rva_count() != 0u32 { return 22u64 }
    let r3b : i32 = rva_admit((&RVA_KAT_OPC as u64) as *u8, 1u64)
    if r3b != RVA_OK { return 23u64 }
    if rva_is_admitted((&RVA_KAT_OPC as u64) as *u8) != 1u8 { return 24u64 }
    return 0u64
}

/* _rva_kat4: admission idempotence.
 * Locals: pp4(1) ii(2) ra1(3) ra2(4) = 4. W13-OK. */
fn _rva_kat4() -> u64 {
    rva_init()
    let pp4 : *u8 = (&RVA_KAT_OPA as u64) as *u8
    pp4[0u64] = 0xAAu8
    let mut ii : u64 = 1u64
    while ii < 32u64 { pp4[ii] = (ii as u8) ; ii = ii + 1u64 }
    let ra1 : i32 = rva_admit((&RVA_KAT_OPA as u64) as *u8, 1u64)
    if ra1 != RVA_OK { return 30u64 }
    if rva_count() != 1u32 { return 31u64 }
    let ra2 : i32 = rva_admit((&RVA_KAT_OPA as u64) as *u8, 1u64)
    if ra2 != RVA_OK { return 32u64 }
    if rva_count() != 1u32 { return 33u64 }
    return 0u64
}

/* _rva_kat5: counterexample reproducibility (M10).
 * Locals: xv5(1) yv5(2) zv5(3) kv5(4) r5a(5) xv5b(6) yv5b(7) zv5b(8)
 *         kv5b(9) r5b(10) same5(11) si(12) = 12. W13-OK. */
fn _rva_kat5() -> u64 {
    rva_init()
    smt_init()
    let xv5 : u32 = smt_bv_new_var(4u32)
    let yv5 : u32 = smt_bv_new_var(4u32)
    let zv5 : u32 = smt_bv_new_var(4u32)
    let kv5 : u32 = smt_bv_new_var(4u32)
    smt_bv_add_eq(xv5, yv5)
    smt_bv_add_eq(kv5, zv5)
    let r5a : i32 = rva_audit_bv_pair(4u32, xv5, zv5)
    if r5a != RVA_E_FAIL { return 40u64 }
    rva_last_counter((&RVA_KAT_CE as u64) as *u8)
    rva_init()
    smt_init()
    let xv5b : u32 = smt_bv_new_var(4u32)
    let yv5b : u32 = smt_bv_new_var(4u32)
    let zv5b : u32 = smt_bv_new_var(4u32)
    let kv5b : u32 = smt_bv_new_var(4u32)
    smt_bv_add_eq(xv5b, yv5b)
    smt_bv_add_eq(kv5b, zv5b)
    let r5b : i32 = rva_audit_bv_pair(4u32, xv5b, zv5b)
    if r5b != RVA_E_FAIL { return 41u64 }
    rva_last_counter((&RVA_KAT_CE2 as u64) as *u8)
    let mut same5 : u8 = 1u8
    let mut si : u64 = 0u64
    while si < 16u64 {
        if RVA_KAT_CE[si] != RVA_KAT_CE2[si] { same5 = 0u8 }
        si = si + 1u64
    }
    if same5 != 1u8 { return 42u64 }
    return 0u64
}

/* ---- rva_selftest: Phase-2 acceptance gate ----
 * Delegates to _rva_kat1.._rva_kat5 (each <=20 locals; W13 fix).
 * Returns 99u64 on full pass (house style).  Returns first failing KAT
 * result on any failure (non-zero return from any sub-helper).
 * Locals: r1(1) r2(2) r3(3) r4(4) r5(5) = 5. W13-OK. */
fn rva_selftest() -> u64 @export {
    let r1 : u64 = _rva_kat1()
    if r1 != 0u64 { return r1 }
    let r2 : u64 = _rva_kat2()
    if r2 != 0u64 { return r2 }
    let r3 : u64 = _rva_kat3()
    if r3 != 0u64 { return r3 }
    let r4 : u64 = _rva_kat4()
    if r4 != 0u64 { return r4 }
    let r5 : u64 = _rva_kat5()
    if r5 != 0u64 { return r5 }
    return 99u64
}

code_changes.anchor: /* 649 -- reversibility_audit: SMT-checked bijection audit + admission of reversible ops. */
code_changes.new_code:
/* 649 -- reversibility_audit: SMT-checked bijection audit + admission of reversible ops.
 * W13 fix (reversibility_audit-w13): _rva_le8_to_u64 replaces inline xb0..xb7/zb0..zb7
 * decode; rva_selftest split into _rva_kat1.._rva_kat5 (each <=20 locals).  Same 99
 * contract: positive arms (KAT1 UNSAT, KAT4 idempotence, KAT5 CE reproducibility) +
 * negative arms (KAT2 SAT->RVA_E_FAIL + x_val!=z_val via _rva_le8_to_u64; KAT3
 * null-cap->RVA_E_CAP).  If _rva_le8_to_u64 is broken (returns 0), KAT2 fires
 * return 12u64 and corpus gate goes RED. */
module corpus_649
extern @abi(c-msvc-x64) fn rva_selftest() -> u64 from "reversibility_audit.iii"
fn main() -> u64 { return rva_selftest() }
